text:
When you run EXPLAIN, PostgreSQL shows estimates, not execution.

Here’s what the main terms mean 👇

Cost
→ Planner’s estimated work
→ Not execution time

Rows
→ How many rows PostgreSQL expects
→ Based on statistics

Width
→ Average size of each row (bytes)
→ Affected by SELECT * vs needed columns

🎯 Interview Insight:
PostgreSQL chooses a plan based on estimated cost, not actual speed.

📌 If estimates are wrong → planner decisions can be wrong.

#PostgreSQL #EXPLAIN #SQLPerformance #DBA #InterviewPrep

text:
Many beginners think SQL performance is only about indexes.
But PostgreSQL decides how to run your query first 👇

PostgreSQL uses a query planner.

When you run:
EXPLAIN

It shows:

How PostgreSQL will scan the table

Whether it uses an INDEX or full table scan

Estimated cost and rows

Without EXPLAIN:
You only see results.

With EXPLAIN:
You see how PostgreSQL thinks 🧠

🔍 EXPLAIN ANALYZE:

Executes the query

Shows actual time & rows

Helps compare estimated vs real performance

💡 Interview Tip:
Always say “I use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to understand query performance”.

📌 One-line takeaway:
EXPLAIN shows the plan. EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows the truth.

#PostgreSQL #SQLPerformance #EXPLAIN #DatabaseInterview #DBALearning

PostgreSQL uses a query planner.

When you use:

EXPLAIN

Shows the planned execution

Tells how PostgreSQL thinks the query will run

Shows estimated cost & rows

Does NOT execute the query

When you use:

EXPLAIN ANALYZE

Executes the query

Shows actual time & rows

Helps compare estimated vs real performance

👉 Without EXPLAIN:
You only see results.

👉 With EXPLAIN:
You see how PostgreSQL thinks 🧠

🎯 Interview Tip:
Always say:

“I use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to understand and tune query performance.”

text:
BACK HANDSPRING -

The Back Handspring is one of those moves that when someone performs it you know that they’re not just good at Back Handsprings, they’re pretty athletic all around.  What makes a person athletic and capable of skills like the Back Handspring? At GMB we believe that the combination of strength, flexibility, and control builds the base to become athletic and more resilient. -

The following progressions demonstrated by GMB Trainer Eduardo @eorihuela will help you with your Back  Handspring training: -

1. Monkey 360 
2. Backwards Monkey 360 
3. Macaco 
4. Standing Macaco 
5. Back Handspring -

Having an experienced coach in a safe place to practice is also very important. You won’t want to just jump into it, you’ll want to develop the attributes and easier skills before attempting these more advanced movements. Things like Bear, Monkey, Frogger are great starters to build up to forward rolls, backwards rolls, Cartwheels, and Handstands.  We wrote an article on the pyramid of preparedness that we think you’ll really like. Check it out: https://gmb.io/athleticism/ -

text:
Did you know? 🔪 Slicing #chicken against the grain can help make the meat more tender 😍💪 But how? 

🍗 This technique is useful especially for tougher cuts like thighs and wings—by breaking the muscle fibers, the meat becomes softer and easier to chew. For thicker cuts, slicing 𝗮𝗴𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘀𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗻 ensures even cooking, preventing some parts from staying raw while others dry out. Plus, it increases the surface area exposed to seasoning or marinade, allowing flavors to penetrate more deeply.

When should you do it? 🤔 
It works well for thighs, wings, and chicken breasts, especially when cooked whole or in large pieces. If you plan to marinate the chicken, cutting against the grain will help it absorb flavors better. It’s important for recipes that require tender meat—if the cooking method might make the meat tough, slicing against the grain is a great way to prevent that.

Did you know it? 👇

text:
How to Prepare Matcha at home 🍵 ₊˚.༄

Here is a beginners guide if you’re new to making matcha. 
1) Bloom your Bamboo Whisk by soaking it in hot water for 2 minutes. This softens the prongs making it easier to whisk later on.
2) Measure 1tsp of ceremonial matcha powder. 
3) Sift! Matcha is prone to clumping over time. 
4) Add in 60ml of hot water (avoid boiling water as this will burn and make your matcha bitter).
5) Whisk away! Use gentle pressure and avoid pressing the bristles hard against the bowl as it may damage your chasen. 
6) For Matcha Latte: pour over 100ml of milk (3.4oz).

And enjoy! 

Let me know if this helped 🫶

text:
How to: Install a Tap

Installing a new tap on your Village Pottery Company ceramic water purifier is quick and straightforward. You will need a shifter or ratchet tool.

Steps

Insert the tap through the hole from the outside of the purifier, ensuring the stainless-steel disc and one white washer are in place.

From the inside, slide on the white washer, the second stainless-steel disc, and the nut.

Screw on the back nut and use a shifter or ratchet to tighten securely.

Check the tap is straight.

Tip
Please see the video in the final IG and FB slide. I find it easiest to hold the nut in place and twist the front of the tap until tight, then repeat while holding the nut with the shifter. This reduces awkward handling and gives a cleaner, firmer fit.

All Village Pottery Company taps and parts are covered by a 1-year warranty. Our taps are made to last (one of ours is still going strong after 10+ years), though wear and tear can vary over time.

If you have any questions or need replacement parts, we’re always happy to help.

Ingredients

2 tbsp olive oil
1/4 cup yogurt

1/2 tsp salt

Instructions
 1. Add avocado, yogurt, lime juice, olive oil, and garlic to a blender.

text:
🍋🐟 ONE LEMON-DILL SAUCE. 6 SALMON DISHES. SAVE THIS.

Creamy, bright, and packed with fresh herbs — this velvety lemon-dill sauce is the perfect match for salmon in every form. From elegant Wellington to simple wraps, this one sauce does it all.

🧄 Lemon-Dill Salmon Sauce

Ingredients
1 cup heavy cream
1/4 cup dry white wine
2 tbsp fresh lemon juice
2 tbsp fresh dill, finely chopped
1 shallot, minced
1 clove garlic, minced
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper

Instructions
 1. In a saucepan over medium heat, sauté shallot and garlic until fragrant (about 1–2 minutes).
 2. Pour in white wine and simmer for 2–3 minutes to reduce slightly.
 3. Add heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer.
 4. Stir in lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
 5. Let sauce simmer for 3–5 minutes until slightly thickened.
 6. Remove from heat and stir in fresh dill.
 7. Serve warm over salmon dishes.

Perfect for: grilled salmon, salmon Wellington, salmon cakes, gnocchi, wraps & more.

text:
What are the things you should look for on a location scout? 

1. I always like to roam around the space with @cadrageapp to find the best frames. This app allows you to type in your specific camera and lens to see exactly how the frame is gonna look on certain focal lengths. 

2. Established where you’re lighting from. Do windows need to be blacked out? Do you need to light the second story? This will help dictate how long it’ll take to set up a certain room and will help you figure out blocking. 

3. Use Sun Seeker to find where the sun is going to be at certain times of day. This helped us specifically when I wanted a certain shot to take place during blue hour. That meant we needed to be facing a certain direction at a certain time of day. This will help you schedule your day.

4. Find out where it’s safe for crew to stash gear, hold talent, have HMU & wardrobe. If you’re shooting in every single room of a location, it’s going to be an absolute nightmare to move everything for every move. Speaking from experience here! 

5. As much as we’d all like our creativity to flourish on the shoot day, they’re just isn’t enough time to have that luxury most of the time. So do your homework, figure it out in preproduction, go to the location beforehand and map out every detail possible.

text:
🔍 The Story Behind This Painting 👇
🎨 "Massacre of the Innocents" by Léon Cogniet (1824)
🏛️ Location: Musée des Beaux-Arts d’Orléans, Orléans, France

This 19th century painting shows a barefoot woman crouched in terror, her child clutched tightly to her chest, her hand covering his mouth. She stares directly at us, eyes wide,not with fear, but with the silent scream of someone watching death approach. Behind her, chaos unfolds as mothers flee down the stairs, soldiers in pursuit.

Biblical Background
This scene refers to the biblical Massacre of the Innocents,when King Herod, fearing the prophecy of a newborn king, ordered all baby boys in Bethlehem under the age of two to be killed. It's one of the darkest and most emotionally charged episodes in the New Testament, a moment of senseless cruelty targeting the most vulnerable.

🔥The Security Council is the most authoritative organ of the UN.

The structure of the Security Council was designed to reflect the global power hierarchy emerging from the Second World War.

The 5 permanent members (P5) were granted special privileges, including the veto power.

The veto power allows a P5 member to block any decision.

The veto power shaped Cold War dynamics, as the US and the Soviet Union often used it to block each other’s interests.

It also undermined the response to conflicts and humanitarian crises in recent years.

Read more about the UN and its Security Council in our recent report, or check out our other pieces, ad-less and free:

text:
This fascinating optical illusion, known as the “Illusion of S*x,” was created by psychology professor Richard Russell in 2009. It demonstrates how our brains use visual cues—specifically contrast—to determine gender. In the image, the two faces are identical, but increasing the contrast (making the eyes and lips darker while the skin stays light) causes the brain to perceive a female face, while lowering the contrast results in a male perception. 

This happens because, biologically, female faces generally have higher natural contrast between their features and their skin than male faces. By artificially manipulating these levels, Russell proved that contrast is a primary “cue” for gender recognition. This research also helps explain why cosmeticsare such a universal human practice; by darkening the eyes and lips, makeup heightens that contrast, subtly emphasizing feminine traits.

Apple's AirPods Pro guide has a step most people completely skip and it is the reason yours keep slipping out or sounding flat.

After you place the tip into your ear canal, you are supposed to rotate the stem toward your face until it sits snug.

That small twist creates a proper acoustic seal. The kind that activates the full bass, locks in the noise cancellation, and keeps them from falling out mid workout or mid commute.

Most people push them straight in and spend years thinking something is wrong with their ears.

The internet is now split half saying "I have done this for years" and half completely blindsided.

Follow @explainingworld we explain the things everyone uses but nobody reads the manual for 🤝

Based on Apple's official AirPods Pro fit guidance.

Poll Question: How were YOU wearing them?

#airpods #didyouknow #explained

text:
Did you know that, according to a popular theory, the bride in 'The Unequal Marriage' was actually the painter Pukirev's crush? Look closely and you'll see him standing behind the bride, angrily glaring at the groom
This captivating scene shows a young woman on the brink of marrying a wealthy prince. But there is more to this painting than meets the eye!
As we dive into the canvas we uncover the artist's own struggles woven into the masterpiece. According to the story, artist Pukirev himself was fresh out of school and was grappling with his passion for painting while his beloved walked down the aisle with another.
Take a closer look at the characters. Notice the discontent etched into the man on the right? Well, that’s because the man is Pukirev. He’s seen accurately showing his inner turmoil at watching his beloved get married to another man. 
And what about the groom? Well, the elderly man with his stiff demeanor hints at troubles ahead and it contrasts sharply with the bride's innocence.
Amidst the celebration though, you’ll also find two mysterious figures lurking in the background. Who are they? Relatives? Ghosts from the past? 
One popular theory is that these women could be the groom's ex-wives very well casting a shadow over the joyous occasion.
 #Explained #artexplained

text:
Fish don’t behave randomly. Every pause, turn, follow, or refusal is tied to energy, safety, and opportunity. When you understand that, fishing stops feeling unpredictable and starts making sense. What looks “weird” to us is usually the most efficient choice for the fish.

A fish sitting still is conserving calories. A fish facing current is letting food and oxygen come to it. A fish that follows your lure without biting isn’t rejecting it — it’s evaluating whether the effort is worth the reward. These behaviors are information, not mistakes.

The better angler isn’t the one who fishes harder or faster. It’s the one who reads behavior and adjusts. Once you start asking why a fish is doing something, you stop guessing — and start learning.

#krausfishing #viral #fishing #weird #explained

text:
On the back of my last post,…when in much of this world we’re consistently given a very different message.

For generations we have been culturally fed a message that our body needs outside support, especially our feet.  Mostly from the footwear industry but that message is actively endorsed by most health professionals in regards to both footwear and a reliance on regular needs for treatment from “health” pros.

Education, or should that be reeducation, is the key.

My daughter was researching her persuasive talk to her class about barefoot shoes and told me that all the podiatrists she looked up are advising people to wear heeled, stiff and cushioned shoes.  She was a bit taken aback by this because of the barefoot and minimalist shoe cocoon that she exists in🤣

The podiatrists are taught this and unfortunately most never question it.

As a podiatrist, it is a privilege to have the opportunity to educate people about how their feet and body function naturally.

That they can move and feel better if they understand how their body is designed to behave.

Whether that be coping with pain, building towards a long hike or performing at an elite sport.

Understanding that if given the right environment your body has all it needs within to heal and perform at its best.

It’s a shock to many of us because we’ve only ever been told very differently.  Slowly but surely … these are changing times in the podiatry and shoe world.

Keep an eye on @thefootcollective for events that I help deliver that are powerful education for everyone with feet and those helping people with feet 😆

#naturalpodiatry #podiatry #selfsupport #empowerment #educate #naturalhealing #healnaturally #strongfeet #footstrong

text:
Childhood sexual abuse (CSA) is not something that “stays in the past.” It can shape a person’s life in ways that aren’t always visible. These experiences are part of what is called “Adverse Childhood Experiences”(ACEs). Early traumas that can have lasting effects on mental, emotional, and even physical health.
The long-term impacts of CSA can include:
• Anxiety, depression, and PTSD
• Difficulty with trust, intimacy, and relationships
• Increased risk of substance use or coping behaviors
• Challenges with boundaries and sense of safety
• Chronic health issues 

Healing is possible, but it can often take time, support and understanding. Awareness and compassion matters! 

#wecanpreventcsa #preventcsa #prevent #educate #foryou

text:
A hidden skill that is vital for both reading and writing! 

It’s called crossing the midline, moving a hand, foot, or eye across the invisible line down our body.

This simple movement helps the left & right sides of the brain work together for smooth reading, writing, and coordination. 

Without it, children might:
⚠️ Switch hands mid-task
⚠️ Lose their place while reading
⚠️ Avoid reaching across their body

The BEST part? You can strengthen it through playful, everyday activities.

👇🏼
Like this post and comment "midline" below and I’ll send you 10 FUN ways to develop this skill.

text:
Mucha gente piensa que un seguro de vida es solo para cuando ya no estés…
pero la realidad es que un seguro de vida permanente es una herramienta para vivir mejor hoy.

Esto no es solo protección…
es protección + ahorro durante toda tu vida.

Primero, garantiza algo fundamental:
👉 Un beneficio por fallecimiento, dinero que protege a tu familia cuando más lo necesitan.

Pero aquí es donde se pone interesante…
👉 También crea valor en efectivo, un dinero que crece con el tiempo y que tú puedes usar en vida.
Puedes pedir préstamos, retirar dinero o incluso ayudarte a pagar tus primas.

Ahora, algo importante que muchos no entienden:
❗ La aportación mensual NO es fija.
Depende de ti… de la cobertura que elijas, de tus metas y de lo que realmente necesitas.
No se trata de un número…
se trata de una estrategia personalizada.

Sí, puede ser más costoso que otras opciones…
pero también te da algo que pocas cosas ofrecen:
✔️ Cobertura de por vida
✔️ Acumulación de dinero
✔️ Protección real para tu familia
✔️ Y acceso a ese dinero mientras estás vivo

La pregunta no es si puedes pagarlo…
la pregunta es:
👉 ¿Puedes permitirte no tenerlo?
Porque al final del día…
no se trata solo de proteger lo que tienes,
sino de asegurar el futuro de los que amas. 💙

1. 🚨Dealing with a defective product? Here's how to fight back!🔧✅ Your rights, return policies, and warranties explained. Thread for empowerment! #ConsumerRights #ProductWarranty

2. 📜Know Your Rights: Consumer protection laws like the Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act ensure retailers and manufacturers honor their products. #ConsumerLaw

3. 🛠️First Steps: Check the return policy and warranty. If within warranty, you're entitled to a repair or replacement. No warranty? You still have implied warranty rights. #Warranty

4. 📞Manufacturer Contact: If the retailer can't help, reach out to the manufacturer for potential repair, replacement, or refund options. #CustomerService

5. 🔄Return It: Within the return period, return the defective product for a refund or exchange, following the retailer's policy. #ReturnPolicy

6. 📜Warranty Wisdom: Understanding express and implied warranties is key to getting your issue resolved. #WarrantyTypes

7. 🚀Alternative Routes: Traditional methods failed? Explore payment protection, extended warranties, lemon laws, and投诉 effectively. #ConsumerAdvocacy

8. 💳Credit Card Protections: Purchases made with a credit card can offer chargeback options if issues persist. #CreditCardRights

9. 🏛️Legal Action: If all else fails, consider legal action. Understand the process and potential outcomes to seek justice. #LegalAction

Selling/transferring Genesis tokens BELOW your original allocation.

- CAN earn by:

- Farm = penalized.

text:
Educational Thread: Multi-Timeframe Analysis Explained

Most traders fail because they use single timeframe analysis. Here's how to do it right:

1. Weekly (HTF): Defines bias
• Bullish if making higher highs/lows
• Bearish if making lower highs/lows
• Sideways if range-bound

2. Daily: Shows momentum
• Confirms weekly bias
• Identifies trend strength
• Shows institutional accumulation/distribution

3. 4H/1H: Entry timeframe
• Where price action happens
• Shows market structure shifts
• Best for identifying setups

4. 15M: Timing perfection
• Entry refinement
• Stop placement
• Risk management

Key insight: Each TF has its own market microstructure. Don't force HTF levels onto LTF charts.

That's why LeoAgent analyzes each TF independently, then shows how they interact. Clarity > Hopium.

No special infra, no headaches  just deploy a Solidity contract and go.

💡 Perfect for: blind auctions, shielded pools.

6/ Encrypted orchestration – run all protocol logic in a single VM.
No more splitting logic between transparent & encrypted environments.
Less complexity, faster dev cycles.

7/ With these, developers can restore the full power of vanilla DeFi  in a fully encrypted environment.
That’s why building on Seismic feels familiar, but delivers confidentiality by default.

8/ If you can build it on Ethereum, you can encrypt it on Seismic.
🚀 Minimal changes.
🔐 Maximum privacy.
⚡ No extra infra.

9/ We’re here to make encrypted DeFi the new default.

Validators and masternodes lock up $BDX to process transactions, support #privacy features, and keep the network stable.

PoS also keeps fees low and transactions efficient, without a mining arms race.

PoS helps decentralize the network.

Anyone who meets the requirements can participate, spreading control instead of concentrating it.

It secures private transactions, powers the ecosystem, and supports long-term sustainability.

That’s PoS done right.

text:
Hi Shawn, Christ dying in place of people is not the same as Christ serving as “penal substitution” for people. Here’s how I just explained it to another person in a thread. 

Let’s say a police officer enters a building to rescue hostages. The kidnappers beat him, torture him, and kill him. His dying words are “forgive them, judge.” At trial, the judge learns of the officer’s dying words and is so moved by the officer’s sacrifice and forgiveness, that he erases the offenders’ sentence if they agree to repent and commit crime no more. This is what Jesus (the officer) and God the Father (the judge) did at Calvary. The police officer is NOT serving the penal sentence in prison for the kidnappers, just as Jesus is NOT serving eternity in hell for us. What Jesus did is sacrifice. It is NOT penal substitution.

text:
#KeralaSurveyFindings2026: ANALYSIS

#BREAKING: TWENTY20 STARES EXISTENTIAL CRISIS IN CENTRAL KERALA.

📌 #EXPLAINER: LET ME BREAK IT DOWN, STEP BY STEP:

[Step 1]
#BACKGROUND & SCENARIO: Twenty20 is a small political group in Kerala, started by a big business company called Kitex Garments. It began as a charity group helping local people with things like cheap food, medicine, and community projects. Over time, it entered politics, promising to stay away from big alliances like the Left (LDF) or Congress-led group (UDF). It focused on being "neutral" and helping ordinary people, especially in rural areas.

[Step 2]

#ARANGETRAM & WHERE THEY STAND TODAY.?

It expanded from its 2015 debut (winning one panchayat) to control four gram panchayats outright, in the 2020 local body elections, marking a strong rural foothold. By 2025, its performance was mixed: it retained control of four gram panchayats but lost two previous strongholds while gaining two new ones. Overall, the party saw a slight increase in total seats won (from around 60+ in 2020 to 78 in 2025), but its expansion into urban areas like Kochi Corporation failed, and vote shares declined in key areas due to opposition alliances. This reflects plateauing influence ahead of the 2026 assembly polls.

[STEP 3]

#INFLUENCE: Twenty20's main power is limited to one area: Ernakulam district in central Kerala. This is where their business is based, and they've built a strong local network through welfare programs like cheap shops and health camps.

[STEP 4]:
#EROSION: Main areas affected: Local bodies (panchayats and blocks) like Kizhakkambalam, Aikkarnadu, Kunnathunad, Mazhuvannur, and Vengola. These are small villages and towns where Twenty20 won elections by promising better daily life (like subsidies on food and clothes).

If leaders quit and take supporters with them, Twenty20 could lose control of these panchayats. For example, in recent local elections, they won seats here, but now resignations could lead to by-elections or shifts in power. Without leaders, they can't run surveys, events, or campaigns effectively in these areas. In short, the loss is mostly local - Ernakulam's rural pockets. The party might shrink back to just being a charity group, not a political force.

📌 Among Which Community Voters Does It Lose Influence?

Twenty20's voters are not from one big group but a mix. They attracted people tired of old parties (LDF and UDF) by focusing on "development" without politics. But their core base is from certain communities, especially minorities who liked their neutral image.

📌 Main communities affected (after joining BJP):

A) Christian voters, especially Jacobite Syrian Christians.
B) Muslim voters.
C) Secular or neutral voters.

Q) Why these communities? Twenty20 promised to dissolve if it ever joined a big alliance. Breaking that promise makes them look dishonest. In #2021_elections, they got about 15% votes in #Ernakulam from these groups. Losing them means dropping to single digits or less.

In simple words:
Christians (especially Jacobites) and Muslims feel tricked (core ideas diluted), while neutral voters feel the party sold out.
